title: Fix procurement incremental MERGE grain — silver and gold merges collide on non-unique keys (crash or silent transaction loss)
description: Both procurement MERGEs use keys coarser than the data's grain, with no pre-merge dedupe. Silver: bronze-to-silver merges on (date, materialname, suppliername) — but bronze grain is 'one row per material purchase' (bronze_tables.md:5), so two same-day purchases of the same material from the same supplier are legitimate and will either throw Delta's 'multiple source rows matched' error (same batch) or silently overwrite each other (cross-run via whenMatchedUpdateAll). The incremental_load_strategy doc specifies a 4-column key including region AND a mandatory dedupe step (doc lines 223-266) — the notebook drops region entirely and never dedupes. Gold: fact_procurement merges on (date_key, material_key, supplier_hq_country_key, production_country_key) — supplier is not in the key, so two same-day transactions from different suppliers HQ'd in the same country collide; the doc anticipates exactly this and mandates aggregation to the merge grain (doc lines 336-344), not implemented. Additionally the gold merge reads the ENTIRE silver table with no p_from_date filter (line 1062), contradicting the doc's filtered gold merge and making collisions certain across full history on every incremental run.
acceptanceCriteria: Silver merge key made truly unique per the strategy doc: either (date, materialname, suppliername, region-or-equivalent) with a documented uniqueness argument, or explicit pre-merge dedupe via row_number() over the natural key ordered by a deterministic tiebreaker (doc incremental_load_strategy.md:254-266 shows the pattern). NOTE: the notebook currently drops the region column (bronze-to-silver:210) — either stop dropping it or justify the 3-column key + dedupe in the doc via /iterate.

Gold fact_procurement incremental path either (a) aggregates to the merge grain per incremental_load_strategy.md:336-344 (SUM quantity_base, SUM spend_eur, AVG unitprice), with the grain change reflected in gold_tables.md, or (b) keeps transaction grain and abandons MERGE for a date-partition overwrite/delete-insert pattern (delete date_key >= watermark, insert replacements). Option chosen is recorded in task notes; if it materially changes the documented fact grain, route the schema-doc update through the docs task.
Gold incremental path filters silver by p_from_date with the 7-day look-back (mirroring bronze-to-silver lines 179-186) instead of reading the full table.

A deliberate duplicate-key test is run against Fabric: inject/craft two same-day rows for the same (material, supplier) in a dev copy of bronze, run incremental, and confirm no Delta multiple-source-match error and no lost transaction (both rows' quantities represented in silver and gold totals).
[Erik/Fabric] Full pipeline run in incremental mode (p_full_load=false) completes end-to-end, and total spend matches a fresh full-load run of the same data (idempotency check).
The bronze duplicate DQ check key is aligned with whatever silver merge key is chosen (see task-026 — if 026 lands first, update the check there; if this lands first, leave a note on 026).
notes: AUDIT-DERIVED (pipeline quality audit 2026-07-13): findings #3 and #4 (critical). Not spec-decomposed — empty drift fingerprints.

EVIDENCE silver: merge condition (date, materialname, suppliername) at bronze-to-silver notebook-content.py:234-245; region dropped at line 210; no dedupe anywhere. Doc contract: incremental_load_strategy.md:223-266 (4-col key + dedup strategy).

EVIDENCE gold: merge_tbl call + condition at silver-to-gold2 notebook-content.py:1157-1166; unfiltered silver read at line 1062; whenMatchedUpdateAll in merge_tbl at lines 82-93. Doc contract: incremental_load_strategy.md:277-344 (filtered read, aggregation note at 336-344). Documented grain 'one row per procurement transaction': gold_tables.md:6.

FAILURE MODES to keep distinct: (1) source batch has 2 rows with same merge key -> Delta runtime error 'multiple source rows matched' (loud, blocks pipeline); (2) row arrives in a later run matching an existing target row that is actually a DIFFERENT transaction -> whenMatchedUpdateAll silently replaces it (silent data loss). A fix must handle both.

AMPLIFIER: task-023's lookup fan-out feeds duplicated rows into this merge — even artificially unique data collides once fan-out duplicates it. Fix order doesn't strictly matter but both must land before incremental mode is trusted.

CONTEXT: with pipeline defaults (p_full_load=false, p_from_date=1900-01-01, no watermark computation — see task-029) every scheduled run takes the MERGE path over full history, so this is the live path, not an edge case.

SYNTHETIC DATA CAVEAT: current demo data may happen to have unique natural keys, which is why this hasn't visibly failed. The audit verified the logic, not a live failure — hence the deliberate duplicate-key test in acceptance.

ROW_ID: source_row_id from monotonically_increasing_id() (silver-to-gold2:1080) is not stable across runs — don't rely on it for merge identity; if a stable row identity is needed for option (b), derive a hash of the full natural row instead.

DECISION 2026-07-14 (Erik, via /work): GOLD = OPTION (b) — keep transaction grain, abandon MERGE for a date-partition delete-insert (delete date_key >= p_from_date window, re-insert cleaned window). Preserves 'one row per transaction' (gold_tables.md:6 UNCHANGED — no doc/semantic-model rewrite needed), keeps per-supplier/per-transaction detail. SILVER = same delete-insert-by-date-window approach for consistency (delete silver_procurement where date >= p_from_date, append cleaned window) — NOT the doc's natural-key-MERGE+dedupe, because dedupe would silently collapse legitimate same-(date,material,supplier) transactions, contradicting the transaction-grain decision. This is a reasoned deviation from acceptance criterion 1's 'dedupe' option; criterion 1's intent (no crash, no silent loss) is still met, losslessly. WATERMARK CAVEAT: task-029 not yet implemented, so p_from_date defaults to 1900-01-01 -> delete-insert currently rewrites full history each run (correct + idempotent, just not incremental-efficient); becomes truly incremental once 029 lands the real high-water-mark. REGION: with delete-insert region is no longer needed as a merge key; keep it only if it has downstream value — document the call. task-026 note: with delete-insert there is no silver 'merge key' collision to guard, so the bronze duplicate DQ check should target genuine data-quality dups, not merge safety (align there).

CLAUDE HALF DONE 2026-07-14: SILVER (bronze-to-silver) incremental branch replaced MERGE with delete-insert — delete silver_procurement where date >= window-MIN, append cleaned 7-day-look-back window (no dedupe; lossless; region drop left intact — repo grep found no downstream reader of silver region). GOLD (silver-to-gold2 fact_procurement) windowed silver read by 7-day look-back, MERGE replaced by delete-insert on date_key >= window-MIN-date_key at transaction grain (no aggregation). KEY REFINEMENT: delete boundary = window's actual MIN date (not literal p_from_date) so the look-back overlap stays idempotent once task-029 lands. merge_tbl() helper now orphaned (harmless). Both files py_compile clean. FR-005 tracks the incremental_load_strategy.md doc reconciliation (delete-insert vs MERGE) -> covered by task-033.

ERIK (Fabric) — remaining acceptance (dev-copy lakehouse): (crit 4) inject two same-day rows for one (material,supplier) into a dev bronze copy, run incremental -> no Delta 'multiple source rows matched' error AND both quantities present in silver+gold totals; (crit 5) full incremental run (p_full_load=false) end-to-end, then RE-RUN the same window -> identical row counts (idempotent), and total spend matches a fresh full-load run of the same data.

NON-BLOCKING (verifier/implementer notes): (1) if a prior FULL load wrote NULL-date rows, switching to incremental leaves them un-refreshed (delete predicate never matches NULL) — they don't duplicate (incremental read filters NULL dates out) but go stale; non-issue for pure-incremental + NULL-free data (task-030 hardens NULL date_key at the source anyway). (2) gold_unmapped_procurement_audit shares fact_procurement_raw and still full-overwrites; it will shrink to the window once task-029 supplies a real watermark — harmless today (1900-01-01 default makes window==full table).

DESIGN HAZARD observed live 2026-07-22 (raised in session, recorded here so it is not lost): the delete-insert is NOT ATOMIC. bronze-to-silver:245-246 runs target_table.delete(...) and then silver_df.write.mode('append'), with no transaction spanning the pair. When the append failed on a schema mismatch, the delete had ALREADY COMMITTED — silver_procurement was left with 0 rows, and each of the 3 pipeline retries repeated the wipe. Any error between the two statements (schema drift, a bad cast, OOM, executor loss) empties the table and leaves it empty.

Recoverable today only because p_from_date defaults to 1900-01-01, making the window the full table so the next successful run rebuilds everything from bronze. That safety net DISAPPEARS when task-029 lands a real high-water-mark: the window narrows, and a mid-operation failure would then delete a date range without re-inserting it — silent partial data loss rather than an obviously-empty table. Sequence this before or alongside task-029.

SAME PATTERN at silver-to-gold2:1285-1290 for fact_procurement (delete on date_key >= window, then append). It has not failed yet but is identical in shape. A partial pipeline success could leave a hollow fact table feeding the report.

Candidate remedies (not yet evaluated): write to a staging table and swap; use Delta MERGE with a synthetic row-identity column so the operation is single-statement and atomic; or replicate the window into a temp table first so a failed append can be rolled forward. Choosing one is a design decision, not a bug fix.

issues.description: Edge case for Erik's awareness (not a blocker): NULL-date rows from a prior FULL load are not refreshed by the incremental delete predicate (date_key>=min never matches NULL); they don't duplicate but go stale. Non-issue for pure-incremental + NULL-free data (task-030 hardens this).
silver-to-gold2 working-tree diff also contains task-023/025 changes (expected — same file, same session); verified intact and non-overlapping with the task-024 fact write path.

task_verification.notes: Claude code-half verified (owner:both). Silver + gold procurement writes replace natural-key MERGE with transaction-grain delete-insert per Erik's Option (b). Idempotency crux verified sound: delete boundary = window MIN date/date_key; every appended row has date>=MIN so delete clears exactly the refilled range; NULL-date duplication structurally prevented (window filter excludes NULL; dim_date built from full silver range; date_key=yyyyMMdd monotonic with date). Watermark default (1900-01-01) resolves to safe full rewrite, not no-op. No aggregation (grain preserved). No dedupe/distinct symptom-hide. Append schema == create schema by construction. Strategy-doc MERGE deviation sanctioned (FR-005). py_compile passes both files. runtime_validation=partial: crit #4 (duplicate-key Fabric test) + #5 (full incremental idempotency run) are Erik's Fabric half; code makes them pass.

verification_history.summary: Transaction-grain delete-insert both layers; idempotency boundary (window-MIN) sound; NULL-date + watermark-default safe; no symptom-hide; py_compile OK. Fabric duplicate-key + idempotency runs = Erik.

test_protocol.summary: Erik's Fabric half — duplicate-key safety + full incremental idempotency (acceptance #4, #5). Run in a dev copy of the lakehouse.

steps.instruction: Inject two rows with the SAME Date/MaterialName/SupplierName (differing e.g. in Quantity) into a dev copy of bronze procurement, run bronze-to-silver + silver-to-gold2 incremental (p_full_load=false).
steps.expected: No Delta 'multiple source rows matched' error; both injected rows present in silver_procurement and both quantities/spend represented in fact_procurement totals (nothing collapsed).

steps.instruction: Run the full pipeline once in incremental mode (p_full_load=false) end-to-end.
steps.expected: Completes Bronze->Gold with no errors; fact_procurement populated.

steps.instruction: Re-run the SAME incremental window a second time (same p_from_date, no bronze changes); compare row counts before/after.
steps.expected: silver_procurement + fact_procurement row counts IDENTICAL after 2nd run (idempotent — no dup, no loss); total SUM(spend_eur) unchanged.

steps.instruction: Compare total spend from the incremental run vs a fresh full-load run (p_full_load=true) of the same data.

test_protocol.automated_results: py_compile passed both notebooks. Idempotency, NULL-date safety, watermark-default full-rewrite, schema-match verified by static analysis. Spark/Delta/Fabric execution = Erik's half.
